text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot with 43 calls and 187 hands needed. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig pays **$53.80/hr plus 10% rollups** on 5-12s Monday-Friday, and Industrial Electric's Lilly LP1 new construction rotates 7-10s with a 13-day rule. Both are inside JW work with real staying power. Book depth here means you're likely first-out if you ink it.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has all four contractors (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein) stacked on the same Port Washington Data Center repost. They're paying **JW wage plus $10/hr base**, which is a straight bonus not contingent on OT — 41 calls, 169 hands. This is the kind of consistent mega-project that doesn't dry up mid-summer.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** shows **$48.00 scale** with Inglett & Stubbs pushing QTS Fayette at **$40** flat on 4-10s and 2-8s Monday-Saturday (Saturday optional). Miller's cable splicer work at the same location bumps to **$40.90** for those with three years documented. Beltline Electric's Plant Yates job in Newnan hits **$42.18** on 5-10s adding three new units. 36 calls, 213 hands — solid board.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is a dark horse: Parsons Electric dominates with 75 calls across three Polaris Forge Data Center postings, all paying **$44.80** with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** plus a **$100-$150/day incentive** (weekends bump to $150). The 100+ mile GPS radius and shift differentials push real money. 122 hands on a small board means quick placement.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** tops out at **$73.42** — Mid City, TSG, and ESI all at that rate on various locations and the Cologix data center. ESI's call specifies all OT paid at double time, 5-10s plus 8 with 40-hour M-F minimums. 23 calls, 117 hands.

Data center construction is consolidating the board: 724 calls across 88 locals means hands are chasing three or four mega-projects (Meta, QTS, Polaris) where mid-scale contractors are stacking multiple postings on the same site.
